free_text: Measurements of low-energy (d,n) reactions for BNCT
measurements were performed at the
88-in. Cyclotron of the Lawrence Berkeley National
Laboratory.
Neutrons were detected by means of five BC501A
liquid scintillator cells with diameter of 12.7 cm
and thickness of 5 cm , coupled to XP2410 Phillips
phototubes. Together with the TOF, the light output
and a standard pulse shape discrimination time for each
event were recorded.
The neutron energy was determined from the
time-of-flight relative to the cyclotron
radiofrequency signal.
Compiled b S.H.
